description: This is going to sound like I work for the company, but I seriously think it's one of the most valuable pieces of software that I've used in as long as I can remember. The ongoing development adds new functionality faster than I can implement it. I recommend it enthusiastically to anyone working with all the different LLMs and every one of them that follows my advice loves using it. I think I used it originally on Setapp for a single day before I bought the Premium version. I am about to switch to the custom version so I can get everyone on my team using it. I haven't done it yet because I'm just being cheap, but that's pretty shortsighted given the productivity boost I expect once I get everyone to start using it.
author: Graydon T.
pros: It's incredibly powerful and flexible. It's like an AI Swiss Army Knife. It allows me to centralize all my interactions with the different models. I really like the ability to setup my own prompts and profiles. There's almost no reason for me to use any other application to interact with all the models out there.
cons: It's a bit complicated to learn, but I think that's more a function of the fact that it can do so many things with so many different models. Any time you have an application that does this much, it's going to be a bit complicated. On the plus side, the documentation is very comprehensive.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Customer Multi-model ChatGPT for your team
description: I'm extremely happy with TypingMind and have recommended it to several people. They care about their users and deliver great support. They ship features fast and are responsive to suggestions. This is an essential product at our company.
author: Justin A.
pros: We use TypingMind Custom which allows me to give all my team members access to AI models from OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, and more - such as GPT-4, Claude 3. Winning features for me: 1. Uses LLM APIs so none of our data is used for training. 2. Access to LLMs from OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, etc 3. In-built RAG (Retrieval Augmented Generation) 4. Robust admin tools to set limits, manage access control (for models, AI characters, training data, etc)
cons: Nothing to dislike about TypingMind. Only drawback is one can't make use of Custom GPTs although recently they did launch the ability to access external APIs which we're yet to test out.

description: The support and communications has been excellent. They routinely update software and release updated models.
author: Anne H.
pros: I like that I don't have to maintain monthly subscriptions with multiple services like ChatGPT, Anthropic, and Google. Instead, I can just access various models using my APIs. I can also set up folders to organize my chats. And there is a mechanism where I can save my prompts or use a community one.
cons: If you clear your browser cookies, you need to reset your software license and any APIs. You can pull in your previous session data. I would like it if I could access some custom ChatGPTs.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Outstanding front-end for chat-based AI systems
description: Outstanding. I've been a user and evangelist from very early on. TypingMind's features, implementation, speed of development, attention to the community, etc. is top notch.
author: Dan O.
pros: Features, ease of use, consistency across models
cons: I have literally no complaints at all!!! Well, the name is kind of silly.

description: It's been very cost effective to pay by token rather than a monthly subscription fee for the different models.
author: Niels P.
pros: I use TypingMind every single day to interact with a variety of different models, e.g. GPT-4-Turbo, Claud3 Opus and Gemini. It's very easy to set up. The interface is clean and intuitive and the cloud sync is great for working across multiple computers. The developer is very responsive and quickly integrates new features as they come out.
cons: It's all good. There is nothing really that needs significant improvements. The developer quickly implements new features.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

author: Ted H.
pros: TypingMind consolidates most of the popular language models into one interface, runs locally if you want it to, and obfuscates the need for monthly subscriptions and their limitations. Using API keys for each of the language models initially allowed me access to models that, for example, OpenAI did not allow without a subscription, and importantly, does so without coding. Constant updates from the developer is a big plus too.
cons: The lack of a native app on mobile initially was a con for me, but using the PWA changed that. I believe that this approach allows the developer to push updates without having to force the end user to download a new version.

author: Verified Reviewer
pros: The BEST interface for generative AI. The sync, profiles, personas, etc make it such a joy to use. The UI and UX are fantastic.
cons: I wish there was a native app version, but the website is great.

description: Amazing. It has been my fav purchase in the past two years. No regret. I purchased a lifetime license
author: Jonathan C.
pros: Ease of use, security, can use multiple models, organization, active community. And the developer keeps shipping updates
cons: The only complaint is that open router models take a few steps to implement but his guides make it easy

author: Blaine M.
pros: TypingMind lets me have a chat-like experience but doesn't limit me to a single API. I can choose whichever one I want for any specific use case. New features are released frequently, and new models available in existing APIs are added within a day if not within a few hours of release.
cons: The plugin system is relatively easy to use, but the interface isn't the most intuitive. (I have not tried writing my own plugins yet.) They must be integrated with ChatGPT even if they are using other APIs behind the scenes.

author: Vignesh J.
pros: I love how simple and user friendly interface!!
cons: Nothing at all! I like everything about it.

review_title: TypingMind - Way better than the OpenAI Playground
author: David K.
pros: It's such a better interface than the playground/api interfaces you get from LLMs. Organized, fast, easy on the eyes. [sensitive content hidden] keeps it updating it regularly too.
cons: I can't really find anything to complain about, except maybe some UI tweaks that would make it easier to work with very large prompts. Overall, it's excellent.
